claim_verdicts.bullet: so optimizations like predicate pushdown, projection pushdown, and expression simplification apply automatically
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Structured Streaming applies most of the optimization rules in Spark SQL
claim_verdicts.issue: Drops qualifier most from source. Source says most of the optimization rules but bullet implies all.

claim_verdicts.bullet: Exactly-once is guaranteed by two persistent logs in the checkpoint directory
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Together, using replayable sources and idempotent sinks, Structured Streaming can ensure end-to-end exactly-once semantics under any failure.
claim_verdicts.issue: Attributes exactly-once to logs alone. Source requires three components: logs + replayable sources + idempotent sinks.
claim_verdicts.suggested_fix: Mention all three components in opening bullet

claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: changelog checkpointing uploads changes made to the state since the last checkpoint for durability
claim_verdicts.issue: Presents changelog as default RocksDB behavior. It is opt-in since Spark 3.3.
claim_verdicts.suggested_fix: Change table cell to Full snapshot default or changelog opt-in Spark 3.3+

claim_verdicts.bullet: Flink latency Sub-millisecond and State Local state RocksDB
claim_verdicts.source_excerpt: Tasks perform all computations by accessing local, often in-memory, state yielding very low processing latencies.
claim_verdicts.issue: Source says very low latencies not sub-millisecond. Source says local often in-memory state not RocksDB.
claim_verdicts.suggested_fix: Change Flink latency to Very low per-event and state to Local often in-memory state
